name: Paralegal
tagline: Cite-check, summarize, and organize case files with skills that keep matter data on your machine.
heroTitle: An AI assistant that does the legwork.
heroSubtitle: Summarize records, build chronologies, and prep filings with reusable skills that run on your own machine. Matter data never leaves your device unless you say so, and every model call is governed, logged, and auditable.
intro: Paralegals carry the document-heavy legwork of a matter: summaries, chronologies, cite-checks, and exhibit prep, all on confidential files that can't go into a public chatbot. Ryu lets you package these repeatable tasks as skills that run locally, ground them on the matter's own documents, and log every call, so the routine work goes faster while an attorney reviews the result.

highlights.title: Reusable skills
highlights.description: Package repeatable tasks like deposition summaries and cite-checks as skills you can run on any matter, the same way each time.

highlights.title: Cites the source
highlights.description: Ground agents on the matter's filings and records with Spaces and RAG, so every summary points back to the page and document.

highlights.title: Matter data stays private
highlights.description: Run a local Gemma 4 on install, or route to a cloud model with PII and DLP redaction on every request.

highlights.title: Auditable by default
highlights.description: Every model and tool call is logged in the Gateway audit trail, so the attorney can see exactly what the assistant worked from.

useCases.title: Document summarization
useCases.description: Condense long filings, deposition transcripts, and discovery into focused summaries with page and line citations to verify.

useCases.title: Chronology building
useCases.description: Assemble a timeline of key events and admissions across the matter's documents, each entry linked to its source.

useCases.title: Cite and quote checking
useCases.description: Check that quotes and citations in a draft match the source record, flagging any that don't line up.

useCases.title: Discovery review and indexing
useCases.description: Run a skill across a document set to extract parties, dates, and key terms into a reviewable, searchable index.

useCases.title: Filing and exhibit prep
useCases.description: Draft an exhibit list, assemble a table of authorities, and organize records into the structure a filing needs for review.

useCases.title: Intake and matter setup
useCases.description: Turn an inbound matter into a structured file with parties, issues, deadlines, and a checklist for the attorney to confirm.

examplePrompts: Summarize this 60-page deposition into the key admissions with page and line citations.
Build a chronology of events from the documents in this matter, linking each entry to its source.
Check every quote and citation in this brief against the record and flag any that don't match.
Extract the parties, dates, and key terms from this discovery set into an index.
Draft an exhibit list and table of authorities from this filing for me to review.

faq.q: Is confidential matter data safe with Ryu?
faq.a: Yes. Ryu is local-first: you can run a model entirely on your machine with no telemetry. When you route to a cloud model, the Gateway redacts PII and sensitive data on egress and logs the call, so matter files stay governed end to end.

faq.q: Will the summaries cite the source?
faq.a: Yes. Ground agents on the matter's documents with Spaces and RAG so summaries and chronologies point back to the specific page and document, letting an attorney verify before relying on them.

faq.q: Can it do the same task the same way each time?
faq.a: Yes. Package a repeatable task as a skill and run it across matters consistently. The work is still a first pass for an attorney to review, and the audit trail records what the assistant worked from.

ctaTitle: Give your team an AI paralegal.
ctaSubtitle: Summarize, organize, and prep faster, with matter data kept private and every call on the record.
ctaNote: Reusable skills · Local-first · Full audit trail
